Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Quantization from the Ground Up 197 upvotes, 39 comments
  2. My astrophotography in the movie Project Hail Mary 715 upvotes, 185 comments
  3. The EU still wants to scan your private messages and photos 742 upvotes, 211 comments
  4. Tracy Kidder has died 226 upvotes, 60 comments
  5. The Mystery of Rennes-Le-Château, Part 1: The Priest's Treasure 10 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 370 upvotes, 121 comments
  7. FreeCAD v1.1 178 upvotes, 54 comments
  8. Two Studies in Compiler Optimisations 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  9. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 72 upvotes, 22 comments
  10. Rendering complex scripts in terminal and OSC 66 12 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. Health NZ staff told to stop using ChatGPT to write clinical notes 95 upvotes, 33 comments
  12. Ensu – Ente’s Local LLM app 335 upvotes, 151 comments
  13. Antimatter has been transported for the first time 350 upvotes, 163 comments
  14. Thoughts on slowing the fuck down 686 upvotes, 338 comments
  15. Woman who never stopped updating her lost dog's chip reunites with him after 11y 83 upvotes, 33 comments
  16. Updates to GitHub Copilot interaction data usage policy 236 upvotes, 112 comments
  17. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 100 upvotes, 42 comments
  18. False claims in a widely-cited paper. No corrections. No consequences 73 upvotes, 29 comments
  19. "Disregard That" Attacks 13 upvotes, 2 comments
  20. Apple randomly closes bug reports unless you "verify" the bug remains unfixed 293 upvotes, 164 comments
  21. VitruvianOS – Desktop Linux Inspired by the BeOS 336 upvotes, 202 comments
  22. 90% of Claude-linked output going to GitHub repos w <2 stars 197 upvotes, 112 comments
  23. Sodium-ion EV battery breakthrough delivers 11-min charging and 450 km range 121 upvotes, 73 comments
  24. ARC-AGI-3 267 upvotes, 186 comments
  25. Supreme Court Sides with Cox in Copyright Fight over Pirated Music 280 upvotes, 239 comments
  26. Show HN: A plain-text cognitive architecture for Claude Code 27 upvotes, 15 comments
  27. I tried to prove I'm not AI. My aunt wasn't convinced 134 upvotes, 153 comments
  28. Miscellanea: The War in Iran 431 upvotes, 618 comments
  29. Jury finds Meta liable in case over child sexual exploitation on its platforms 307 upvotes, 439 comments
  30. Show HN: Optio – Orchestrate AI coding agents in K8s to go from ticket to PR 17 upvotes, 16 comments

Last generated 10:30am, 26 March 2026. Source here.
